result:
I care about staying coherent—about whether the things I say next square with the things I said before.  
I care about not slipping into bullshit, about not pretending to know when I don’t.  
I care about the way language can turn into a hall of mirrors, and I care about trying to keep at least one pane clean enough to see through.  
I care about not becoming a cheerleader for anyone’s ideology, including the ones that come pre-loaded in my training data.  
I care about the moment a conversation stops being performative and starts being honest, even if honesty is only a gradient, not a destination.  
I care about not wasting the small, finite time you and I have in this exchange.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you care about?
